spec_natural_language: function transferOwnership(address) MUST revoke DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE from the old owner when ownership is transferred

violated_invariant.precondition: msg.sender is current owner and newOwner is a valid non-zero address
violated_invariant.expected_behavior: After ownership transfer completes, the old owner should no longer have DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE and thus should not be able to call grantRole() or revokeRole()

extraction_reasoning: The finding clearly describes that transferOwnership() fails to revoke DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE from the old owner. The invariant is unambiguous: after ownership transfer, the old owner should lose DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE privileges. The affected function is the inherited transferOwnership() which needs to be overridden to handle role revocation.
